PROVISIONS OF THE YESSENOV LAUNCH PAD COMPETITIVE PROGRAM
1. GENERAL TERMS

1.1. The YESSENOV LAUNCH PAD Program (hereinafter referred to as the ‘Program’ provides up to ten
(10) grants for additional training aimed at developing hard skills and/or soft skills, limited to no more
than two (2) online/offline training courses, with a duration of up to twelve (12) months.

12. The Program is funded at the initiative of the Private foundation ‘The Shakhmardan Yessenov
Science and Education Foundation’ (hereinafter referred to as the ‘Foundation).

1.3. The Program is aimed at developing hard and soft skills that enable individuals to address
professional and life challenges at a higher level of quality.

1.4. In these Provisions, the term ‘Grant’ refers to financial support of up to six hundred thousand
(600,000) tenge awarded to the Program winners, who demonstrate the highest level of interest and
determination in pursuing additional education.

1.5. The winners of the Program are selected through an open Competition.

|. PROGRAM IMPLEMENTATION

2.1. The Program is long-term and is implemented in several stages:

2.1.1. The first stage is the competitive selection of the Program winners;

2.1.2. The second stage - the winners of the Competition independently search for online/offline training
courses to develop hard and/or soft skills;

2.1.3. The third stage is the training of the Program winners.
2.2. For conducting a competitive selection, the Foundation establishes an Expert commission, which
selects participants. The Expert commission selects scholarship recipients (winners) on the basis of the
results of two (2) rounds of the Competition.
2.3. The decision of the Expert commission is approved by the Foundation’s order which contains
officially certified list of the Program winners. The list of the Program winners is to be published on the
Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org.
2.4. Applications for participation in the Program are accepted in the Kazakh, Russian and English
languages. The working language of the Program is Russian.

2.5. The Foundation has the right to refuse to provide funding for the Program training without
explaining the reasons for the refusal.

2.6. The Foundation awards up to ten (10) grants under the Program. The final decision on the
number of the Program grants is determined by the Foundation's Expert Commission.

2.7. The Foundation reserves the right to award fewer or more than ten (10) declared grants, should the
Foundation’s Expert Council make such a decision.

Ш. REQUIREMENTS FOR CANDIDATES

3.1. Only citizens of the Republic of Kazakhstan who are over eighteen (18) years of age, penultimate and
final year undergraduates, master’s students, doctoral students and working professionals in the
following areas* can participate in the Program:

- natural sciences, mathematics and statistics;

- information and communication technologies;

- engineering, manufacturing and construction industries;

- healthcare.

* according to the Classifier of Training Programs for Personnel with Higher and Post-Graduate
Education (Approved by the Order of the Minister of Education and Science of the Republic of
Kazakhstan as of October 13, 2018, No. 569).

The following qualities of applicants will be taken into consideration in determining the Program winners:
3.1.1. high intellectual potential;

3.1.2. willingness to develop hard skills;

5.1.5. a broad range of hard skills

3.1.4 focusing on efficiency;

3.1.5. desire and ability to take responsibility for the quality of his/her own life.

3.2. The candidate must declare and confirm the desire to improve his/her hard skills in the areas
specified in clause 3.1. of these Provisions, as well as justify the need to develop soft skills:


3.2.1. project management;

3.2.2. professional communication and negotiation;
5.2.5. presentation and public speaking mastery;
3.2.4. stress management;

3.2.5. time management;

3.2.6. emotional intelligence;

3.2.7. leadership and project team management;
5.2.8. others, in coordination with the Foundation.

3.3. To participate in the Competition, a candidate must:

5.5.1. Complete the online application form on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org.

5.5.2. Attach the following documents to the application:

Required documents:

1) scan of the identity card;

2) an essay explaining why he/she needs additional skills and how they will affect his/her professional
and personal life;

3) resume/CV.

Additional documents:

1) a portfolio showcasing the candidate’s achievements and/or expressed interests in a specific area, in
accordance with clause 3.1. of these Provisions, and provide relevant evidence — no more than three (3)
documents.

2) recommendations — no more than one (1) document;

3) certificates confirming academic achievements, success in research and/or engineering activities,
participation in conferences, seminars of different levels - no more than three (3) documents*;

4) individual or co-authored publications - no more than two (2) publications;

5) diploma (last place of study), if available - no more than one (1) document;

6) social activities and volunteering certificates — no more than two (2) certificates*

*The Foundation considers a limited number of certificates. The candidate must independently
select the certificates that best showcase his/her achievements.

3.4. Applications are accepted on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org from September
10 through October 14, 2025.

The deadline for acceptance of applications is October 14, 2025, 3:00 P.M. Astana time (GMT+5).
Applications received after the deadline will not be considered.

3.5. An application and essay can be submitted in the Kazakh, Russian or English languages.
Documents (certificates, diplomas, recommendations, etc.) can be provided in their original
language.

3.6. Submission of documents to participate in the Competition implies that the applicant shares the
goals of the Program, confirms his/her agreement with all the terms and conditions of the Program as
set forth in these Provisions, and is prepared to fully comply with them without any exceptions.

IV. STAGES OF COMPETITIVE SELECTION

4.1. The Competition consists of two (2) rounds. The best candidates are selected after each round.

4.2. The First round of the Competition is an application assessment.

All the applications received on time are reviewed and assessed by the Foundation’s employees and
independent experts from October 15 through October 27, 2025, in accordance with the following
criteria:

4.2.1. portfolio quality;

4.2.2. essay quality;

4.2.3. work experience;

4.2.4. participation in olympiads, conferences, exhibitions, seminars;

4.2.5. availability of publications;

4.2.6. foreign language proficiency;

4.2.7. social activities.



The results of the first round of the Competition will be communicated to the candidate via email. Тһе
list of candidates who have passed to the second round of the Competition is to be published on the
Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org - no later than October 28, 2025.

4.3. Based on the results of the first round of the Competition, no more than twenty (20)
candidates are selected.

4.4. The second round of the Competition is an online individual and/or group interview.
The online interview is conducted by the Foundation’s employees and a psychologist from October 29
through November 7, 2025.

4.5. The following candidate qualities are assessed during this interview:

4.5.1. hard skills;

4.5.2. learning ability and the ability to quickly perceive and analyze new information;
4.5.3. determination;

4.5.4. the ability to adapt to a new situation and find non-standard solutions;

4.5.5. communication skills;

4.5.6. clarity and reasonableness of goal setting;

4.5.7. emotional intelligence;

4.5.8. others

4.6. The Foundation reserves the right not to comment on its decisions to the participants of the
Competition.

4.7. The Foundation makes a final decision on November 10, 2025. The list of the Program winners is to
be published on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org.

4.8. Based on the results of the second round of the Competition, ten (10) winners of the Program
are selected.

V. SPONSORSHIP TERMS AND CONDITIONS

5.1. The Foundation assists the Program winners in finding high-quality further education courses to
enhance their hard and/or soft skills.

5.2. The Program winner must coordinate the selected courses with the Foundation. The Foundation
may engage an expert to evaluate the quality of the courses.

5.5. To undertake the selected courses, the Competition winner and the Foundation shall enter into a
Sponsorship Agreement (hereinafter referred to as the ‘Agreement’), which serves as the basis for the
Foundation’s disbursement of funds (Sponsorship assistance).

5.4. The Competition winner awarded an education grant must comply with the terms of the Agreement
concluded with the Foundation, including but not limited to:

1) attend courses according to the schedule and not miss classes without a valid reason;

2) responsibly complete all coursework and independent assignments as part of the training course;

3) take exams on time;

4) comply with all other obligations and rules of the educational institution where the Program winner is
enrolled in a training course;

5) disseminate information about the Foundation in any appropriate form that supports a positive
reputation of the Foundation, including through his/her own social media accounts, as well as distribute
information and advertising materials provided by the Foundation;

6) inform the Foundation about their personal achievements in hard and soft skills;

7) participate in blogs and other proposed activities as and when requested by the Foundation.

8) provide any information, including that related to participation in the Competition and the
implementation of the awarded grant (internship, scholarship, training, etc.), as and when requested by
the Foundation, no later than three (3) business days from the date the request is received at the email
address specified in the application;

9) provide assistance in the Foundation’s activities and programs as and when requested by the
Foundation;

10) provide personal photos, text, and video materials (from places of study/work/internships/social
events, etc.) as and when requested by the Foundation (people’s appearance and clothing in photos

3



and videos must Бе пеа{ апа appropriate for a secular setting), as well as participate іп photo апа
video sessions during the Foundation’s events, with the purpose of creating success stories for the
Foundation’s platforms and other publications aimed at motivating the youth of Kazakhstan;

11) in performing their obligations under the Agreement, the Program winner and the Foundation’s
employees shall refrain from committing, encouraging any actions that violate or contribute to the
violation of the legislation of the Republic of Kazakhstan, including in the field of combating corruption.
They shall not pay, offer to pay or authorize the payment of any money or valuables, directly or
indirectly, to any persons to influence their actions or decisions in order to obtain any unlawful
advantage or achieve other improper objectives.

5.5. For all questions regarding the Program and/or the Provisions, please contact the authorized
representatives of the Foundation:

- Aigerym Sultan: aigerim.s@yessenovfoundation.org, phone: +7 701 627 9607:
- Irina Grishchenko: irina.g@yessenovfoundation.org, phone: +7 771 775 1815.

5.6. The Foundation reserves the right to verify, by any legal means, the information provided by
applicants and/or winners of the Program and to exclude any applicant from the Competition at any
round and/or the Program in the event that the information is found to be inaccurate or false.

5.7. The Foundation reserves the right not to provide explanations regarding the motives and reasons
behind decisions concerning applicants for the Program grants.

PROVISIONS
OF THE SHAKHMARDAN YESSENOV FOUNDATION
“Academician Shakhmardan Yessenov Scholarship Competition 2026”

|. General Terms.
1.1. The Academician Shakhmardan Yessenov Scholarship Competition (hereinafter referred to as the
“Competition”) is held and financed at the initiative of the private foundation “Shakhmardan Yessenov
Science and Education Foundation” (hereinafter referred to as the “Foundation”), and is aimed to
stimulate the academic, scientific, and social activities of the citizens of the Republic of Kazakhstan
studying at Kazakhstani higher education institutions (HEls), in order to perpetuate the memory of
Shakhmardan Yessenov - an outstanding geoscientist, prominent statesman and public figure,
academician of the National Academy of Sciences of the Republic of Kazakhstan, doctor of geological
and mineralogical sciences, professor, Lenin Prize laureate, laureate of the State Prize of the Republic of
Kazakhstan, and Shokan Ualikhanov Prize laureate.
1.2. The Competition aims to promote the growth of professional and personal competencies, social
responsibility, and leadership skills among the best undergraduate students at higher education
institutions in the Republic of Kazakhstan.
1.3. In the Provisions, the term “Scholarship” refers to a monetary payment from the Foundation in the
amount of seventy thousand (70,000) tenge per month or eight hundred forty thousand (840,000)
tenge per year. The Scholarship is awarded by the Foundation to no more than twenty (20) Scholarship
holders (winners) of the Competition for a period of twelve (12) months, from May 2026 through April
2027.
1.4. The Scholarship holders (winners) are selected by the Foundation through an open Competition. The
Competition is held by the Foundation in the second semester of the current academic year. The
Foundation awards named Scholarships based on the Competition results.
1.5. The Program provides for a mandatory quota of two (2) Scholarships for students of Satbayev
University (KazNRTU named after К. I. Satpayev) where Academician Shakhmardan Yessenov studied
and taught.

|. Requirements for applicants for the Academician Shakhmardan Yessenov Named Scholarship.
2.1. The Competition is open to citizens of the Republic of Kazakhstan over the age of eighteen (18) who
are full-time students at Kazakhstani higher education institutions, whether on a state-funded or self-
funded basis.
2.2. Applicants for the Scholarship Competition may be:
a. undergraduate students, except those in the first and final years of study;
b. bachelor’s degree students at medical higher education institutions, except those in their first year of
study, if an internship is mandatory;
studying at Kazakhstani higher education institutions, according to the classifier of areas of
training for personnel with higher and postgraduate education* as provided by the legislation of
the Republic of Kazakhstan, in the following areas:
- 6805 natural sciences, mathematics and statistics;
- 6BOG information and communication technologies;
- 6807 engineering, manufacturing and construction industries;
- 6B10 healthcare.
Twenty (20) Scholarship winners will be selected from among the applicants.
2.3 Applicants studying in a bachelor’s degree program must have academic performance of “good” or
“excellent” (the average GPA must be at least 3.0). The GPA data of the applicants must be confirmed by
a transcript.
2.4. The Scholarship applicant must declare and provide documentary evidence of active participation in
the scientific and social life at their HEI.
2.5. Any Scholarship applicant who is a relative of a member of the HEI’s administration (such as the
Rector, Vice-Rector, member of the Academic Council, Institute Director, Dean, or Head of Department)



where the applicant studies must disclose this familial relationship in their application for the
Competition.

2.6. The awarding of the Scholarship does not affect the applicant’s right to receive a state scholarship,
including an increased scholarship.

2.7. The Competition is held annually. An applicant who has previously received the Scholarship also has
the right to participate in the Competition in subsequent years on general terms.

Ill. Procedure for awarding the Scholarship.
3.1. The Scholarship is awarded to no more than twenty (20) students - winners of the Competition,
studying in the specialties listed in paragraph 2.2 of the Provisions, from among those who submitted
applications to participate in the Competition, in accordance with paragraph 3.6 of the Provisions.
5.2. An Expert Commission is established to conduct the Competition and selects the Scholarship
holders (winners) based on the results of two (2) rounds of the Competition.
3.3. The decision of the Expert Commission is approved by an order of the Foundation in the form of an
officially certified list of the Competition winners. The results of the rounds and the list of winners are
published on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org.
3.4. The Scholarship is awarded by the Foundation once (1) a year.
3.5. To participate in the Competition, the applicant has to:
3.5.1. fill out an online application form оп the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org;
3.5.2. attach the following documents to the application:
Required documents:
a. scan of the identity card;

*Order of the Minister of Education and Science of the Republic of Kazakhstan as of October 13, 2018, No. 569 "On
approval of the Classifier of areas of training of personnel with higher and postgraduate education" (registered in
the Ministry of Justice of the Republic of Kazakhstan on October 17, 2018, No. 17565)

Б. an essay on the topic “Is it easy to become a professional in the modern world? What hinders апа
what contributes to this?” The essay should not exceed 750 words (Send only in an editable Microsoft
Word document); and must be written in the language of the application form — Kazakh or Russian;

c. the student’s transcript (the average GPA must be at least 3.0). Applications with a GPA below 3.0
are not considered;

а. a certificate of no criminal record/ criminal record.

Additional documents:

a. science certificates/diplomas — no more than two (2) certificates*;

Ы. social activities and volunteering certificates — no more than three (3) certificates*;

с. publications — no more than two (2) publications*;

4. recommendation letter from the scientific supervisor/nead of department — no more than опе (1)
document.

*The Foundation considers a limited number of certificates. The candidate must independently choose
the certificates that best showcase their achievements.

3.6. Applications are accepted on the Foundation’s website yessenovfoundation.org from February 2
through March 3, 2026.

The deadline for acceptance of applications is March 3, 2026, 3:00 P.M. (GMT+5).

Applications received after the deadline will not be considered.

3.7. By submitting an application on the website yessenovfoundation.org, the candidate confirms that
they have read and understood the terms of the Program, consents to them, and affirms their readiness
to comply fully with these terms. By applying for participation in the Competition, the applicant
automatically confirms to the Foundation their consent to the processing of their personal data by the
Foundation.



3.8. Тһе Scholarship is named апа cannot be transferred from one student to another.

3.9. Submission of the documents for the Competition means that the applicant shares the goals of the
Program and agrees with all the terms of the Competition procedure set out in these Provisions and is

ready to fully comply with them.

3.10. An application can be submitted in two (2) languages: Kazakh or Russian.

3.11. The Foundation reserves the right not to provide explanations regarding the motives and reasons

for its decisions concerning the applicants for the Scholarship Competition.

3.12. The Foundation provides no more than twenty (20) Scholarships, which are awarded based on the
decision made by the Expert Commission of the Competition.

№. Stages of competitive selection.

4.1. The Competition comprises two (2) rounds. The best applicants are revealed at the end of each
round.

4.2. The first round of the Competition is held from March 3 through March 18, 2026.

4.2.1. All applications received are reviewed and assessed in absentia by independent experts according
to the following criteria:

a. high academic potential (including erudition in the specified area of interest; novelty of approaches;
good academic results);

b. availability of scientific publications related to the speciality;

c. activity in student life;

d. volunteer experience;

e. other.

4.2.3. Applicants who advanced to the second round of the Competition receive an email letter. The list
of those qualified for the second round of the Competition is also published on the Foundation’s website
yessenovfoundation.org. Upon completion of the first round of the Competition, no more than fifty (50)
students will be selected. The results of the first qualifying round of the Competition will be announced
no later than March 20, 2026.

4.3. The second round of the Competition is held from March 26 through April 7, 2026.

4.3.1. The applicants selected for the second round of the Competition are invited to an online selection,
which is conducted as an individual interview and/or group case work. During this interview, the
applicants’ qualities are assessed through the observation of the following areas:

a. general communication;

b. system of values and attitudes, motivation;

c. general activity;

d. emotional intelligence;

e. understanding of the demands of the modern world;

f. overall psychological maturity;

g. thinking;

h. willingness of applicants to post motivating content on social media.

4.3.2. The duration of the individual interview is up to forty (40) minutes, and group work is up to one
hundred and twenty (120) minutes. If necessary, the Foundation may conduct additional testing of the
applicants. At the end of the second round of the Competition, no more than twenty (20) winners will be
selected.

4.3.3. The Foundation reserves the right not to comment on its decisions to the Competition
participants.

4.4. The list of the Scholarship holders (winners) of the Competition is published on the Foundation’s
website yessenovfoundation.org no later than April 8, 2026.

V. Terms of receiving and payment procedure of the Scholarship.
5.1. The Foundation enters into a sponsorship agreement with the Scholarship holder (winner) of the
Competition.


5.2. The recipient who has entered into a sponsorship agreement with the Foundation is obliged to
comply with the terms of the agreement, including, but not limited to:

1) to study at a higher education institution in accordance with its rules and requirements;

2) to keep up with studies at a higher education institution in all disciplines getting “good” and
“excellent” grades. As and when requested by the Foundation, academic performance data must be
confirmed by a transcript that the Recipient provides within five (5) calendar days from the date of the
request;

5) to actively participate in student scientific and practical conferences;

4) to be actively involved in social activities at the higher education institution;

5) to disseminate information about the Foundation in any appropriate form, including posts on their
social media accounts, while maintaining and promoting a positive image of the Foundation, and to
distribute informational and promotional materials provided by the Foundation;

6) to inform the Foundation of their achievements in professional activities and soft skills development;
7) to participate in blogs and other proposed activities upon the request of the Foundation;

8) to provide any information, including that related to participation in the competition and the
implementation of the awarded grant (internship, scholarship, training, etc.), upon the Foundation’s
request, no later than three (3) business days from the moment the request is received at the email
address specified in the application;

9) as and when requested by the Foundation, provide assistance in its activities and programs;

10) to provide personal photos, text, and video materials (from places of study, work, internships, social
events, etc.) upon request of the Foundation (ensuring that individuals in the photos and videos
appear neat and appropriately dressed for a secular environment), and to participate in photo and
video sessions during the Foundation’s events for the purpose of creating success stories for the
Foundation’s platforms and other publications aimed at inspiring the youth of Kazakhstan;

11) in performing their obligations under the Agreement, the Program winner and the Foundation’s
employees shall refrain from committing, and encouraging any actions that violate or contribute to the
violation of the legislation of the Republic of Kazakhstan, including in the field of combating corruption.
They shall not pay, offer to pay or authorize the payment of any money or valuables, directly or
indirectly, to any persons to influence their actions or decisions in order to obtain any unlawful
advantage or achieve other improper objectives.

5.2. The Scholarship is paid based on the agreement concluded between the Foundation and the
Scholarship holder (winner) of the Competition, by transferring funds to the Scholarship holder’s
(winner’s) bank account no later than the fifteenth (15th) day of the following month.

5.3. The Scholarship is paid monthly and only to active students at the higher education institutions in
the Republic of Kazakhstan. The Scholarship payment to the recipient (winner) of the Competition is
terminated by the Foundation if the student is on academic leave, etc., the sponsorship agreement
between the specified parties is considered terminated unilaterally on the initiative of the Foundation.
5.4. The Foundation reserves the right to verify the information provided by applicants and/or winners of
the Program by any legal means and to exclude any applicant from the Competition at any stage and/or
from the Program in the event that such information is found to be inaccurate.

5.5. The Foundation reserves the right not to provide explanations regarding the motives or reasons for
decisions made concerning grant applicants under the Program.

5.6. For all questions regarding the Competition and the Provisions, please contact the authorized
representative of the Foundation:

- Aigerim Aidarqyzy Sultan: phone number +7 701 627 96 07; email address:
aigerim.s@yessenovfoundation.org.

REGULATION
on Scholarship Competition of Acamedic Shakhmardan Yessenov
Science and Education Foundation

1. General Provisions

1.1. The scholarship competition (hereinafter - the "Competition") is held and funded at the initiative of the
Acamedic Shakhmardan Yessenov Science and Education Foundation, hereinafter referred to as the
Foundation, is aimed at stimulating the educational, scientific, social activities of students in the natural
sciences, technical, medical and IT fields and perpetuating the memory of Shakhmardan Yessenov, an
outstanding scientist geologist, a prominent statesman and public figure, academician of the National
Academy of Sciences, Doctor of Geological and Mineralogical Sciences, Professor, Lenin Prize Laureate,
State Prize of the Republic of Kazakhstan, Laureate of the Prize named after C. Valikhanova.

1.2. The purpose of the Competition is to promote the development of science in the Republic of
Kazakhstan by supporting the best undergraduate and graduate students from leading universities in the
country and developing their competencies demanded by modern community.

1.3. In the Regulation, the “scholarship” means a cash payment in the amount of 324,000 (three hundred
twenty four thousand) tenge to 25 (twenty five) fellows for a period of 12 (twelve) months. Scholarships
are paid from May 2020 to April 2021, for students of 2-3 undergraduate courses, 1 graduate course, 1
internship, which study in the specialties listed in paragraph 2.1. Only 25 (twenty five) scholarship winners
are selected from among the contestants.

1.4. Winners are selected during the open Competition. The competition is held once a year, in the
second semester of the academic year. Students, citizens of Kazakhstan over 18 years of age, full-time
education, both on the basis of state scholarship and contract, can take part in the competition. According
to the results of the Competition, scholarships are awarded.

1.5. The program involves a mandatory quota for scholarships by 2 (two) students from KazNITU.
Historically, the 2 best students of the university, where Academician Sharkhmardan Yessenov taught
and where his office is located, always had the opportunity to receive scholarships.

|. Requirements to applicants for a scholarship of Shakhmardan Yessenov.
2.1. The scholarship applicant may be a student studying at a Kazakhstan university in natural sciences,
technical, medical and IT specialties.

2.2. Applicants studying in the undergraduate and graduate programs should be have “good” and
“excellent” grades in the disciplines (average GPA score is not lower than 3.0). Data must be confirmed
by transcript.

2.3. The applicant must declare and confirm documented active participation in the scientific, public life of
his/her university.

2.4. A student who is a relative (as defined below) of the leaders of the university, where he/she is
studying (Rector, Vice-Rector, member of the Academic Council of the University, Director of the Institute,
Dean, Head of the Department) must indicate these family ties in the application for the Competition.

2.5. The award of a scholarship does not cancel the student’s right to receive a state scholarship,
including an increased scholarship.

2.6. The competition is held annually. A student who once received a scholarship can each time
participate in this competition on a general basis.

Ш. Scholarship award procedure.

3.1. Scholarship of Shakhmardan Yessenov is awarded to 25 (twenty-five) students from among the best
students of the 2-3 year undergraduate courses, 1st year of master's program, 1st year of internship
studying in the specialties specified in paragraph II, which meet the following requirements:

a) have “good” and “excellent” grades for the current year of study (average GPA score not lower than
3.0);


b) actively participate in student scientific and technical conferences;
с) actively participate in the public life of the institute and the university.

3.2. To conduct the competition, an Expert Commission is created, which selects fellows based on the
results of two rounds of the Competition.

3.3. The selection of students for a scholarship named of Shakhmardan Yessenov is carried out by the
Expert Commission based on the results of two rounds described below. The decision of the Expert
Council is approved by the order of the Foundation in the form of an officially certified list of winners of the
scholarship of Shakhmardan Yessenov. The results of the tours and the competition are published on the
website of the Foundation (yessenovfoundation.org) and on Facebook, Instagram, Telegram.

3.5. The scholarship is awarded once a year and is paid for 12 months from May 2020 to April 2021.
3.6. To participate in the Shakhmardan Yessenov Scholarship Competition, a candidate must:

1. Fill out the online application form on the website of the Foundation — yessenovfoundation.org

2. Attach the following to the application form:

Required documents:

2.1. ID card photocopy;

2.2. Statement titled “What have | done and am doing for the science in Kazakhstan? What is
important for me and why?” (the statement may be filed only in writing);

2.3. Transcript of a student (average GPA score not lower than 3.0), applications with the score below
3.0 are not considered;

2.4. Copy of a Diploma of Higher Education for the students of the master’s program (bachelor,
specialist).

Supplementary documents:

2.5. Scientific Certificates/Diplomas - not more than 2 certificates”;

2.6. Certificates for social activity and volunteering - not more than 3 certificates *;

2.7. Publication— not more than 2 publications*;

* The Foundation considers a limited number of certificates. The candidate must independently select the
certificates that best reflect his/her achievements.

3.7. The applications are accepted on the website of the Foundation from 20 January to

20 February 2020.

Acceptance of applications will end on February 20, 2020. at_15:00 according to the time of Nur
Sultan (GMT + 6). Applications received after the deadline are not considered.

3.8. By submitting an application for participation in the competition, the student automatically confirms
his/her consent to handling personal data.

3.9. The scholarship is registered and cannot be transferred from one student to another.

3.10. Submission of documents for the Competition means that the applicant shares the goals of the
program and confirms his/her agreement with all terms for the Competition set forth in this Regulation and
is ready to fully comply therewith.

3.11. Applications are accepted in 3 (three) languages: Kazakh, English, Russian.

№. Competitive selection stages.
4.1. The scholarship competition consists of 2 (two) rounds, at the end of each of these rounds the best
students are selected.

4.2. The first round is held from 24 February to 10 March 2020.:
1) All applications received on time are reviewed and evaluated in absentia by independent experts
according to the following criteria:
* high academic potential (including erudition in the designated area of interest; novelty of
approaches; good academic results);
availability of scientific publications related to the specialty;
activity in student life;
volunteer experience;
and other.


2) Тһе average GPA transcript score must be а least 3.0.

3) Applicants who have passed in the second round receive an email. Lists of those who passed in the
second round are also published on the Foundation's website and on social networks. At the end of
the first round of the scholarship competition, no more than 50 students are selected. The results of
the first qualifying round will be announced no later than March 11.

4.3The second round is held 25-31 March 2020:
1) Participants in the second round are invited to face-to-face selection, which is held in the form of a
business game. In the process of this game, the following qualities of students are evaluated by
independent experts and psychologists on a five-point scale:
„ leadership skills;
teamwork skill;
clarity and reasoning when setting tasks;
learning ability and ability to quickly perceive and analyze new information;
organizational skills;
communication skills;
ability to adapt to a new situation and find innovative solutions;
patriotism;
knowledge of Kazakh, English and Russian languages;
other.

2) The duration of the business game is 4 hours. The competition is held in Almaty or Nur-Sultan. At the
end of the second round of the scholarship competition, 25 students are selected. If necessary, the
Shahmardan Yessenov Foundation may conduct additional testing of applicants.

3) Participants of the second round are paid for travel to the venue for full-time round-trip tours (train
ticket), as well as accommodation in the city of selection (no more than one day). Place of residence is
determined by the Foundation.

4.4. The list of winners of the Scholarship Competition is published on the Foundation's website before
5 April 2020.

V. Scholarship award and payment terms.
5.1. A sponsorship agreement is concluded with the Scholarship Winner.

5.2. The scholarship is paid on the basis of an agreement concluded between the Shakhmardan
Yessenov Foundation and the student by transferring money to the student’s bank account no later than
the 15th day of the following month.

5.3. The scholarship is paid monthly only to valid university students, if the student is on academic leave,
etc., the scholarship is ceased.

For all questions, please contact by email:
Nurmukhanov.n@yessenovfoundation.org

PROVISIONS
OF THE SHAKHMARDAN YESSENOV FOUNDATION PROGRAM
“RESEARCH INTERNSHIPS IN WORLD LABORATORIES — 2026”

1. GENERAL TERMS

1.1. The “Research Internships in World Laboratories -2026” Program (hereinafter referred to as “the
Program”) of the private foundation “The Shakhmardan Yessenov Science and Education Foundation”
(hereinafter referred to as “the Foundation”) provides ten (10) grants to the winners of the Program for a
research internship in the leading scientific and research laboratories in the world for a period of up to
ninety (90) calendar days.

1.2. The Program is designed to support talented Kazakhstani students and current research scientists
who are willing to pursue a PhD degree and engage in scientific researches in the Republic of Kazakhstan.

1.5. The Program is aimed to increase the competitiveness of Kazakhstani students and researchers
when applying for master’s and PhD programs by providing internship opportunities in the best scientific
and research laboratories in the world.

1.4. Only citizens of the Republic of Kazakhstan studying at Kazakhstani universities at the following
levels can participate in the Program:

1.4.1. undergraduate students, except those in their first and final years of study;

1.4.2. master’s students, except those in their final year of study;

1.4.3. bachelors at medical higher education institutions, except those in their first year of study, if
internship is mandatory;

1.4.4. interns, except those in their final year of study, according to the classifier of areas of training of
personnel with higher and postgraduate education* provided for by the legislation of the Republic
of Kazakhstan, in the following areas:

1) natural sciences, mathematics and statistics;

2) information and communication technologies;

3) engineering, manufacturing and construction industries;

4) healthcare.

*Order of the Minister of Education and Science of the Republic of Kazakhstan as of October 13, 2018,
No. 569 "On approval of the Classifier of areas of training of personnel with higher and postgraduate
education" (registered in the Ministry of Justice of the Republic of Kazakhstan on October 17, 2018, No.
17565)

Il. PROGRAM IMPLEMENTATION STAGES
2.1 The Program is a long-term project which is implemented in several stages:
The first stage is the competitive selection of the Program winners;

The second stage is preparation for the internship by the winners of the Program:

1) the winners of the Program independently search for research supervisors and laboratories around the
world to complete their internship within the framework of the Program;

2) the winners of the Program prepare the corresponding package of documents, consisting of: essays,
recommendations, translation of scientific papers, etc.;

3) the winners of the Program independently search for accommodation for the period of the internship in
world laboratories and get a visa to the country of the internship (if a visa is required).

At this stage, the Foundation provides consulting support to the winners of the Program.

The third stage is doing an internship by the winners of the Program;
The fourth stage is the submission of a financial (in the form according to Appendix No. 1 to these

Provisions) and performance report (in the form according to Appendix No. 2 to these Provisions) by the
winners of the Program.


2.2. For the competitive selection of the Program participants, the Foundation establishes an Expert
Council, which carries out the selection of the Program participants. The decision of the Expert Council is
submitted to the Head of the Foundation for consideration.

2.5. The decision of the Head of the Foundation is approved by an order in the form of an officially
certified list of the winners of the Program. The list of the Program winners is published on the

Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org.

2.4. Applications for participation in the Program are accepted in the Kazakh, Russian and English
languages. The working language of the Program is Russian.

2.5. The Foundation has the right to refuse to provide funding for the internship under the Program
without citing reasons for the refusal.

2.6. The Foundation has the right to independently redistribute grants between undergraduate and
graduate students, and research scientists depending on the number of applications received and the

results of the competitive selection.

2.7. The Foundation reserves the right to provide fewer or more than the ten (10) announced grants, if the
Foundation’s Expert Council so decides.

Ш. COMPETITIVE SELECTION OF THE PROGRAM PARTICIPANTS

3.1. Requirements for the Program Participants:

5.1.1. The candidate must be a citizen of the Republic of Kazakhstan residing in the territory of the
Republic of Kazakhstan;
5.1.2. The candidate could be:

1) a current research scientist (with a degree not higher than a master’s degree), working at a Kazakhstani
higher education institution, research institute, academy, laboratory, etc.;

2) a student in the 2-3 year of undergraduate degree;

3) a student in the 2-5 year of undergraduate degree at medical higher education institutions where
internship is mandatory;

4) a 1-year master’s student;

5) a non-final year internship student studying exclusively at a Kazakhstani higher education
institution or a specialist working at a Kazakhstani institution in the following areas:

- natural sciences, mathematics and statistics;

- information and communication technologies;

- engineering, manufacturing and construction industries;

- healthcare.

3.1.3. The candidate’s GPA should be 3.3 or higher.

5.1.4. The candidate must have achievements in various scientific olympiads and competitions;

5.1.5. The candidate must have an English proficiency level of at least B1 level and prove language
proficiency by:

1) providing IELTS certificate— a minimum score of 6.0;
2) providing TOEFL iBT certificate— a minimum score of 80;
3) education in the English language.

5.1.6. The candidate must have a strong interest in a specific scientific area and provide the following
relevant evidence:

1) personal scientific publications,
2) results of any research activities,
3) other.

5.1.7 The candidate must demonstrate an interest to pursue a PhD degree and a wish to be engaged in
scientific researches in the Republic of Kazakhstan.

5.2. To participate in the Program selection process, the candidate has to:

5.2.1. complete the online application form on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org.
5.2.2. attach to the application the following

Required documents:

1) scan of the identity card;



2) essay on the topic “Research | would like to conduct”. The essay should contain answers to the
following questions:

a. What research experience have you got so far?

b. What kind of a research are you looking forward to conduct, and what could be done to make it
happen? (send an essay only in an editable Microsoft Word document);

3) transcripts (all semesters of study) or a bachelor’s degree diploma with grades (for master’s students
and research scientists), or a diploma from the last place of study - no more than one (1) document;

4) curriculum vitae.

Additional documents:

1) certificates in science — no more than three (3) certificates*;

2) social activities and volunteering certificates - no more than two (2) certificates”;

3) publications — no more than two (2) publications*;

4) recommendation letter from the scientific supervisor/dean of the faculty/vice-rector/rector of the

university — no more than one (1) document.

*When reviewing additional documents, the Foundation considers а limited number от
certificates/publications provided by the candidate. The candidate must independently choose the
certificates/publications that best showcase his/her achievements.

3.3. Applications are accepted on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org from October 6
through November 6, 2025. The deadline for acceptance of applications is November 6, 2025, 3:00
P.M. Astana time (GMT+5).

Applications received after the deadline will not be considered.

3.4. An application and essay can be submitted in the Kazakh, Russian or English languages. Documents
(certificates, diplomas, recommendations, etc.) are accepted in their original language.

5.5. Submission of documents to participate in the Competition implies that the applicant shares the
goals of the Program, confirms his/her agreement with all the terms and conditions of the Program as set
forth in these Provisions, and is prepared to fully comply with them without any exceptions.

5.6. Grant holders of the Foundation’s Research Internships in World Laboratories Program are not
eligible for the 2026 grant.

IV. STAGES OF THE PROGRAM SELECTION PROCESS

4.1. The Program selection process consists of two (2) rounds. The best candidates are selected after
each round.

4.2. The First round of the Program selection process is an application assessment:
All the applications received on time are assessed by the Foundation employees and independent experts
engaged by the Foundation during the period from November 7 through November 24, 2025.

4.2.1. The candidates’ applications are assessed in accordance with the following criteria:

1) the candidate’s academic performance;

2) the quality of the application;

3) the candidate’s scientific and social achievements;

4) the level of interest in the Program; the level of interest in further scientific activity.

4.2.2. The list of the participants selected for the second round will be published no later than November
25, 2025, on the Foundation’s official website: yessenovfoundation.org.

4.3. Upon completion of the first round of the Competition, no more than forty (40) participants are
selected.

4.4. The second round of the Program selection process consists of an individual interview with the
candidate and a group interview, conducted online by a trainer-psychologist and the Foundation’s
employees.

Candidates who have passed the first (1st) stage of the Program selection process are eligible for the
interview.

During the interview, the following qualities of the candidates are assessed:

1) leadership skills;


2) clarity and reasonableness of goal setting;

3) learning ability and ability to quickly perceive and analyze new information;

4) communication skills;

5) ability to adapt to a new situation and find non-standard solutions;

6) business qualities;

7) politeness and tact;

8) determination;

9) flexibility and ability to accept other people’s points of view;

10) self-criticism, ability to acknowledge his/her mistakes and admit them;

11) emotional intelligence level;

12) level of empathy;

13) and others.

The interview, designed to assess the personal qualities of the candidates, will be conducted from
November 26 through December 12, 2025. The Foundation reserves the right to send test assignments
to the Program participant after the interview assessment.

4.5. Candidates who have successfully passed the competitive selection process become winners of the
Program.

4.6. The Foundation reserves the right not to provide explanations for the motives and reasons behind its
decisions regarding the allocation of the Program’s grants.

4.7. The list of the Program winners will be published on the Foundation’s website
yessenovfoundation.org no later than December, 15, 2025.

V. SPONSORSHIP PROCEDURE

5.1. Grants to the winners of the Program are awarded based on a sponsorship agreement concluded
between the Foundation and the Program winner.

5.2. The grant covers the following expenses:

1) round-trip airfare;

2) visa and health insurance costs;

3) hotel quarantine (up to two (2) weeks) if required in the destination country;

4) accommodation and utility costs;

5) food and other expenses (approved rate: forty (40) US dollars per one (1) day, from the date of actual
arrival at the internship location, for a total period of no more than ninety (90) calendar days;

6) transfer along the route: airport (host country) - accommodation - airport (host country).

The total amount of the grant should not exceed four million seven hundred (4,700,000) tenge.

5.5 The grant recipient who has concluded the sponsorship agreement with the Foundation is obliged to
comply with the following terms of the agreement:

1) to complete the internship within the established timeframe and in accordance with the host
laboratory’s rules;

2) to responsibly fulfill all professional requirements of the internship;

3) to promptly report any major issues encountered during the internship (financial, organizational,
logistical, pandemic-related, etc.);

4) during the internship period, publish at least two (2) posts related to the internship on his/her personal
social media accounts, using the Foundation’s designated hashtag (#) and tag (@);

5) to keep in touch with the Foundation’s representatives during and after the internship;

6) to contribute to the writing of his/her success story by providing photographs that illustrate the
internship process and his/her experience in the laboratory;

7) to inform the Foundation about the results of the internship by submitting the relevant reports
(performance and financial).

8) to submit the relevant financial and performance reports in accordance with the deadlines specified in
the agreement. The forms of financial and performance reports are specified in Appendix No. 1 and
Appendix No. 2 to these Provisions;

9) to disseminate information about the Foundation in any appropriate form, including posts on his/her
social media accounts, while maintaining and promoting a positive image of the Foundation, and to
distribute informational and promotional materials provided by the Foundation;

4


10) to inform the Foundation of his/her achievements іп professional activities and soft skills
development;

11) to provide any information, including that related to participation in the competition and the
implementation of the awarded grant (internship, scholarship, training, etc.), upon the Foundation’s
request, no later than three (3) business days from the moment the request is received at the email
address specified in the application;

12) to participate in blogs and other proposed activities upon the request of the Foundation;

13) to provide personal photos, text, and video materials (from places of study, work, internships, social
events, etc.) upon request of the Foundation (ensuring that individuals in the photos and videos
appear neat_and appropriately dressed for a secular environment), and to participate in photo and
video sessions during the Foundation’s events for the purpose of creating success stories for the
Foundation’s platforms and other publications aimed at inspiring the youth of Kazakhstan;

14) in performing their obligations under the Agreement, the Program winner and the Foundation’s
employees shall refrain from committing, and encouraging any actions that violate or contribute to the
violation of the legislation of the Republic of Kazakhstan, including in the field of combating corruption.
They shall not pay, offer to pay or authorize the payment of any money or valuables, directly or indirectly,
to any persons to influence their actions or decisions in order to obtain any unlawful advantage or achieve
other improper objectives.

5.4. The Program winner is solely responsible for obtaining any required vaccinations, as mandated
by the conditions of the country where the internship takes place.

5.5. For all questions regarding the Program and/or the Provisions, please contact the authorized
representatives of the Foundation:

- Irina Grishchenko: email: irina.gq@yessenovfoundation.org,
phone number: +7 771 775 18 15;
- Aigerym Sultan: email: aigerim.s@yessenovfoundation.org,
phone number: +7 701 627 96 07.

5.6. The Foundation may request from the higher education institution where the winner of the Program is
studying any additional information and/or documents concerning the winner of the Program (grant
recipient).

5.7. The Foundation reserves the right to verify the information provided, by all legal means, and to

exclude any grant applicant from participation in the Program if the information and/or documents
provided do not correspond to reality.

text:
Provisions of the “2017 Summer internships т UK research laboratories” program
of the Shakhmardan Yessenov science and education Foundation

1. General provisions

1.1. The research internship program is initiated by the Shakhmardan Yessenov science and education
Foundation (hereinafter referred to as the Foundation) for students and specialists (hereinafter the
Program) and aims to identify, select, and support talented Kazakhstani scientists and researchers
interested in pursuing science long-term and who are also eager to contribute to developing science
and Kazakhstan.

1.2. Henceforth, the term “sponsorship” means a grant in the amount defined individually paid to each
Program participant in Kazakhstan tenge for the period of not exceeding three (3) months that covers a
round trip ticket, visa, standard insurance, accommodation and daily meals on ground. Grant is provided
on a gratuitous basis to each of Grantees of this Program to undertake a research internship in a UK
research laboratory;

1.3. Ву initiating and rendering the present Program, the Foundation believes that the experience gained
from these internships will have a positive impact on forming country’s pool of talented scientists;

1.4. Тһе Program is carried out and financed by the Shakhmardan Yessenov science and education
foundation;

1.5. This Program offers the opportunity for the top 5 (five) students shortlisted in the course of a two-stage
competitive selection to earn a grant for a research internship in a UK laboratory in the summer of 2017;

1.6. Тһе Program is open to undergraduate, graduate and postgraduate students enrolled in universities of
Kazakhstan with majors in chemistry or oil and gas disciplines, as well ав practicing specialists who
already have a degree in the above-mentioned fields. All prospective applicants must be over 18 years
old and citizens of the Republic of Kazakhstan.

2. Stages of the Program implementation:

2.1. It is along-term Program and there are several stages of its realization;
2.2. Stage |: Competitive selection of the Program participants. At this stage, we will select the best students
who demonstrate:
e excellent knowledge on main subjects,
е asound interest in further scientific activity,
e scientific and other such achievements (Olympiads, conferences, researches),
e a sufficient command of the English language;
2.3. Stage ІІ: a preparatory stage and enrolling to the research internship. At this stage, the participants who
have passed the Stage | selection start with preparing for the internship in the UK:
1) compiling the necessary set of documents to obtain visa;
2) obtaining an insurance;
3) searching for accommodation;
4) contacting with the professor regarding the research work;
At this stage, a team of the Program’s consultants shall advise and assist the Program participants (please,
see Article 5 of the present Provisions).

3. Competitive Selection of the Program Participants:

3.1. Requirements to the participants:

1) Eligibility: (1) citizens of Kazakhstan over 18 years old; (2) practicing specialists in the fields
of chemistry and oil and gas and (3) undergraduate, graduate and postgraduate students
enrolled in one of the Kazakhstan universities majoring in:

= Chemistry;

Environmental studies;

Bioengineering;

Oil and gas;

Materials science and technology of new materials;

Chemical technology of inorganic substances;

Chemical technology of organic substances;

HSE;

Petrochemicals;

Nanomaterials and nanotechnologies;

other related specializations.

2) Competitors must have a well and above academic record (“good” and “excellent” marks)
especially in the main subjects for the current academic year. Grades must be confirmed by
the original and a copy of the student’s record book (GPA) and accompanied with a
recommendation letter(s) from the President/Vice President of the student’s school or
research advisor;


3) Competitors must have achievements іп the Olympiads and scientific projects. Data must be
confirmed with copies and originals of diplomas and certificates and any of their
attachments;

4) Competitors must have a good command of the English language, that is, at least an upper
intermediate level and above. In case a competitor cannot provide relevant certificates
confirming the language proficiency, they will be given the opportunity to pass a tests and/or
an interview;

5) Competitor must demonstrate strong interest in specific fields of science and provide
relevant evidence to the Foundation, for example, his/her own scientific articles, materials,
results of any research, etc.;

6) Furthermore, Competitor has to show interest and desire in pursuing science and scientific
activity in the long-term. With respect to this, the Competitor must write an essay to support
his motivation and choice.

3.2. Required documents

Eligible candidates must submit the Application filled out in English to the Foundation no later than April 23,
2017. In addition to the application, the following documents must be provided:

CV;

А copy of applicant’s national ID;

A copy of applicant’s grades record (with all the examination periods) or a copy of diploma;
Copies of diplomas, certificates, scientific projects, еіс.;

An essay on the subject: “I want to undertake this research internship because...”
Recommendations from university’s President/Vice President or research advisor;

English language certificate (IELTS/ TOEFL/other).

NOOARWN>

The application form contains a list of professors and their current research. Applicant must choose from this
list one professor whose research topic he/she is interested in working on during the internship should the
applicant be one of the 5 (five) grantees.

All of the above listed documents shall be submitted in the English language only. Application and all
the required documents must be sent to the Foundation’s email: info@yessenovfoundation.org.

3.3. Тһе competition procedure:

Competition is held in two rounds:

1) Applications assessment:
All applications received are verified and evaluated by independent experts based on availability of
scientific papers on major-related topics, level of interest and reasons for his/her continuation and desire
to be engaged in scientific activity, the English language level, and completeness of application. In case a
competitor cannot provide a relevant document stating English language proficiency, he/she will be given
the opportunity to pass a telephone interview in English.

No more than 3 candidates for each professor are selected following the results of the first competition
round.

2) Selection by professors:
The list of the applicants who successfully pass the first round of the selection will be sent to the
professors of a partner university in the UK — the Open University. Professors shall invite the participants
of the second round for a telephone or Skype interview at their discretion.

A maximum of five (5) winners are selected from amongst the first round winners. The results of the
competition will be announced on the Foundation’s website no later May 23, 2017.

4. Team of the Program Consultants:

4.1 Тһе team of the Program consultants includes members of an initiative group of young Kazakhstani
scientists studying at the universities around the world;
4.2 Duties of the consultants:

1) Consulting Program Participants іп a timely manner;

2) Consultants render only the consulting/advising help and do not fill out and/or write any
forms, applications, essays for the Program participants, nor do they take any other actions
which can jeopardize the competition process of the Program;

4.3 Consultants assist gratuitously on their own initiative;

4.4 Тһе Foundation has the right, both on paid and gratuitous basis, to engage trainers and psychologists to
carry out the necessary trainings to develop and enhance additional skill sets, knowledge and abilities of
the Program’s participants.


5.

6.4

Competitive Selection of the Program Grantees

The Program participants who receive invitations from a research laboratory for the internship
automatically become the participants of the final competitive selection to receive the Grant for the
internship trip;

Grants will be provided to the best five (5) participants who, in the course of preparation, prove
themselves as the most effective, determined, and motivated scientists;

The decision on provision of Grant shall be made jointly (the Contest Committee) by the designated
representatives of the Foundation, representatives of the Program consultants team, trainers, and
experts;

The list of the Competition winners is published officially on the Foundation’s website after it has been
approved by the Founder and agreed by the Contest Committee and the members of the board of
trustees;

The Foundation reserves the right to refuse the Grant without providing explanation the reason of such
refusal.

Provision of Grant

The grant is provided to the winners of the competition subject to a grant agreement;
The grant covers a round trip ticket, visa, standard insurance and living expenses (accommodation and
food) of the Program Participant for the research internship in the host laboratory. Daily food and
transport allowance is 40 GBP;
The grant can be paid by means of a money wire to the Grantee’s bank account or in cash directly to
the Grantee;
The Grantee who has signed the grant agreement is obliged to meet the following terms and conditions
of the Agreement:
1) ю undertake the research internship in compliance with the established rules of the foundation
and the host laboratory;
2) to deliver his/her work responsibly in the course of the research internship program;
3) to inform the Foundation on a regular basis on progress of the internship by means of providing
relevant reports.

Additional information to the Provisions:

1.

The research internship program is designed for the following purposes:
- to give the opportunity for scientists to complete internships in laboratories of leading world
universities and scientific centres;
- to provide incentives for innovation in oil and gas and geology in Kazakhstan;
- to increase the qualifications of Kazakhstani students and scientists by exposing them to
qualitatively different conditions of learning at world’s leading universities.

Provisions of the Program of Research Internships т World Laboratories т 2018
1. GENERAL PROVISIONS

The research internship program initiated by the Shakhmardan Yessenov Science and Education
Foundation (hereinafter referred to as the Foundation) for students who wish to pursue doctoral
degree in world universities (hereinafter the Program), aims to identify, select, and support
talented Kazakhstani students interested in science on a long-term basis.

Henceforth, the term Grant means a monetary payment of no more than 7 000 USD paid in
Kazakh tenge at a rate on the day of payment, provided on a gratuitous basis to each of Grantees
of this Program to undertake a research internship in a research laboratory in the world;

In our understanding, a Ph.D is a must for talented individuals to enable them to pursue scientific
and research activities successfully. As such, initiating and rendering the present Program meets
the Foundation’s goals to form and enhance national pool of talented scientists;

The Program is carried out and financed by the Foundation;

This Program offers the opportunity for the top ten students shortlisted in the course of a two-
stage competitive selection to earn a grant for a research internship in laboratories around the
world in the summer of 2018;

The Program is open to sophomore and junior students majoring in engineering and natural
sciences, and enrolled in universities of Kazakhstan and who are also citizens of the Republic of
Kazakhstan;

2. STAGES OF THE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE PROGRAM:

The Program is long-term and realized in several stages;

Stage |: Competitive selection of the Program participants. At this stage, we will select the best

students who demonstrate:

1) excellent knowledge on main subjects,

2) asound interest in further scientific activity,

3) achievements in scientific activities (Olympiads, conferences, researches),

4) a sufficient command of English;

Stage Il: a preparatory stage and enrolling to the research internship placement. At this stage, the

participants who have passed the competitive selection stage start with preparing and getting

placements with the US laboratories:

1) search for several potential suitable programs;

2) compile necessary set of documents (1.е., essays, referral and recommendation letters,
translations of scientific works, etc.);

3) initiate and maintain contact (correspondence) with professors;

4) await results from potential research labs;

At this stage, a team of the Program’s consultants shall advise and assist the Program participants
(please, see Article 5 of the present Provisions).

3.

COMPETITIVE SELECTION OF PROGRAM PARTICIPANTS:

3.1 Requirements to the participants:

1)

2)

3)

Eligibility: (1) 274 or 3 year bachelor students іп one of the Kazakhstani universities, majoring in
engineering and natural sciences, and (2) citizens of Kazakhstan;

Competitors must succeed on all main subjects with “excellent” and "good" marks of the current
academic year. Data must be confirmed by the original and copy of the student’s record book;
and availability of recommendations from the President or Vice President of the student’s
institution;

Competitors must have achievements in the Olympiads and high school scientific projects as well
as a high average score of their certificate of secondary education. Data must be confirmed with
copies and originals of diplomas and certificates and its attachments;


4) Competitors must һауе а good command of the English language, that is, at least an intermediate
level and above. In case a competitor cannot provide relevant certificates stating the language
level, he/she will be given the opportunity to pass relevant tests;

5) Competitor must demonstrate strong interest in specific fields of science and provide relevant
evidence to the Foundation, for example, his/her own scientific articles, materials, results of any
research, etc.;

6) Moreover, Competitor has to show interest in pursuing a PhD and a desire to be engaged in
scientific activity in the long-term. With respect to this, the Competitor will have to write an essay
describing the justification of this choice.

3.2 Grantees of the 2017 Program are not eligible to apply for the 2018 Program.
3.3 The ccompetition procedure:

Competition is held in two stages:
1) Submitting Applications
- Students submit filled out Application to the Foundation till 10 December 2017. The following
documents must be provided:
1. Acopy of student’s national ID;
A copy of student’s grades record (with all the examination periods);
Copies of diplomas, certificates, scientific projects, etc.
An essay on the subject: “I would like to receive a Ph.D because...”
Recommendations of the Univeristy’s President/ Vice-principal/ Research Advisor.

оьом

- Application and all the required documents must be sent to the Foundation’s email:
info @yessenovfoundation.org

- At this stage, the selection is made based on the level of the student’s academic progress,
completeness of Application, availability of achievements, level of interest and reasons for his/her
continuation and desire to be engaged in scientific activity.

2) Interview/trainings:

- Students who submitted the application in timely manner and passed the first stage of selection will
be invited for trainings/interview;

- Interview and the training are designed to explore applicants’ personal qualities;

- Interview/training will take place on 10-20 January 2018.

3.4 Those who pass this competitive selection become the participants of the Program and move to
the next Preparatory stage.

4. PREPARATORY STAGE AND ENROLLMENT

4.1. The Program participants who have passed the Competitive selection have the opportunity to work
with trainers and consultants of the Foundation to get necessary recommendations and information to
enroll with one of laboratories’ study placements programs;

4.2. Consultants of the Foundation conduct master classes and trainings on the following:

- joint search and selection of appropriate study placement programs;

- compiling and submitting relevant documents;

- correspondence with professors of universities and laboratories;

- general trainings on enhancing leadership skills, stress resistance, and communication skills;

4.3. In the course of this work, Program participants undertake to send the required documents and
requests to enroll to research internships with laboratories around the world;

4.4. Following all of this work and in due time Program Participants shall report to the Foundation with
their results on the process.


5. TEAM OF THE PROGRAM CONSULTANTS

5.1. The team of the Program consultants consists of members of an initiative group of young scientists
of Kazakhstan who are now working or studying in the USA or around the world;

5.2. Duties of the consultants:

- Timely provision of master classes for Program Participants;

- Carrying out not less than 5 (five) consultations for each Program Participant;

- Whenever possible, assistance and participation in process of submitting study placement applications
by Participants;

- Consultants render only the consulting/advising help and do not fill out and/or write any forms,
applications, essays for the Program participants, nor do they take any other actions which can
jeopardize the process of the Program;

5.3. Consultants assist gratuitously on their own initiative;

5.4. The Foundation also has the right, both on paid and on a gratuitous basis, to engage trainers and
psychologists for carrying out the corresponding trainings develop and enhance additional skills and
knowledge of the Program participants.

6. COMPETITIVE SELECTION OF THE PROGRAM GRANTEES

6.1. The Program participants who receive invitations from the laboratories for the research internship
automatically become the participants of the final competitive selection to receive the Grant for the
trip;

6.2. Grants will be provided to the 10 (ten) best participants who, in the course of preparation, prove
themselves as the most effective, determined, and motivated students;

6.3. The decision on provision of Grant shall be made jointly (the Contest Committee) by the designated
representatives of the Foundation, representatives of the Program consultants team, trainers, and
experts;

6.4. Competition winners list, approved by the Foundation’s Founder and agreed by the Contest
Committee and the members of the Board of Trustees, is published officially on the Foundation’s
website;

6.5. The Foundation reserves the right to refuse the Grant without providing explanation the reason of
such refusal.

7. PROVISION OF GRANT

7.1. The grant is provided to the winners of the competition by a grant agreement;
7.2. The grant covers both travel and living expenses (accommodation and food) of the Program
Participant for the research internship in the laboratory that agrees to host the winner;
7.3. The grant can be paid by mean of money wire to the Grantee’s bank account;
7.4. The Grantee who has signed the grant agreement is obliged to meet the following terms and
conditions of the Agreement:
1) to undertake the research internship in the prescribed form in compliance with the established
rules of the host laboratory;
2) to deliver his/her work responsibly in the course of the research internship program;
3) to inform the Foundation on a regular basis on progress of the internship by means of providing
relevant reports.

Additional information to the Provisions:

1. A PhD is a scientific degree awarded to graduates of doctoral study programs in the western
education system, and is an analogue of the domestic candidate of science. The degree has no
practical relation to philosophy (only historical) and is awarded almost in all scientific areas, for
example: the doctor of philosophy in literature or the doctor of philosophy in physics.

2. The research internship program is designed for the following purposes:


-to give the opportunity for students to enroll and complete internships in laboratories of leading
world universities and scientific centers;

-to increase the level of preparation of domestic scientists by exposing them to qualitatively
different conditions of studies at the world research laboratories.
